entry.title: Where can I start learning functions and customizing settings of pricing within SAP S/4HANA Sales?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring Pricing in SAP S/4HANA Sales learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will introduce you to the functions and customizing settings of pricing within SAP S/4HANA Sales.

Learning objectives
At the end of this learning journey, application consultants and business users will be able to customize and use pricing for SAP S/4HANA Sales, ensuring efficiency and accuracy in sales pricing mechanisms.
Participants will be able to  maintain condition records, will learn to exploit special pricing functions and condition types, including tax determination, and to analyze and adapt the configuration of pricing in their own SAP S/4HANA system.
They will also learn about other related topics like managing condition contracts effectively.

 Prerequisites

  • Applying SAP S/4HANA Sales
Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Help! Why Don’t I Get a Notification for My Custom Situation?
entry.content:
You’ve configured your own situation use case using the Extended Framework for Situation Handling. Now you’re waiting for a notification but nothing happens. What could have gone wrong?

Let me ask you a few questions to check whether your configuration is correct.

  • Has a situation occurred?
  • How have the conditions been set up?
  • Are all situation triggers covered?
  • Is recipient determination working?
  • Have email notifications been set up correctly?

These questions can support you as an SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP S/4HANA customer.

 

Has a situation occurred?

This may seem trivial but depending on the conditions that have been defined for the situation type, a situation instance may not have been created. An easy way to find out is for the user is to check the My Situations – Extended app to see if there’s an instance that they should have been notified about. If the instance is not displayed in the app, continue checking the conditions. If there’s an instance but no notification was sent, check the notifications / life cycle settings.

List of situaitons in My Situations – Extended app

Or, as a key user, you can display the current situation instances for a situation type by choosing Manage Instances in the header of the Manage Situation Types – Extended app.

Manage Instances in Manage Situation Types - Extended app

In the list of situation instances you can see if a notification was sent and when. Depending on the configuration, a notification can be sent only when a situation occurs for the first time or each time the situation is updated. If you don’t see an entry here, no notification was sent.

List of situation instances in Manage Situation Types - Extended app

There’s also the Monitor Situations – Extended app for key users that shows all created situation instances and all activities for an instance. You need to have situation monitoring enabled for the situation type in the Situation Instances section.

Enable the monitoring in Manage Situations - Extended app

List of situation instances in the Monitor Situations - Extended app

If no situation instance is displayed in the Manage Situation Types – Extended app and in the Monitor Situations – Extended app, this means that the users did not receive a notification because there no situation instance was created.  

If you expected a situation based on the conditions that you have defined, you may find clues in the following section on conditions.

If a situation instance is displayed but no notification was sent, check the notifications / life cycle setting.

How have the conditions been set up?

In the Conditions section of the Manage Situation Types – Extended app, let’s look at the settings for Notifications / Life Cycle, the Order of the conditions, and the Filters.

Notifications / life cycle settings

Each condition can have a different Notifications/Life Cycle setting. Make sure you’ve assigned the appropriate ones.

Notification setting

You can select from three standard options:

  1. The notification is sent to all recipients when a situation is created. When the situation is updated, only the assigned processors are notified.
  2. No one receives a notification after a situation is closed.
  3. All recipients receive a notification after a closed situation has been reopened (this option is available only for object-based situations with the instance closing behavior Close & Keep)

If you’ve defined your own Notification/Life Cycle behavior, I recommend double-checking the life cycle transitions. Make sure you have considered all relevant transitions from creation to closure. And check the notification settings for each transition.

Consider the processing order of conditions

If you’ve set up multiple conditions, the order of those conditions is important. The order defines the sequence in which the conditions are executed. It’s important to understand that the processing of the conditions stops when the first condition in the processing order is met. Conditions lower in the sequence are ignored. For this reason, you need to ensure that the more specific conditions, for which you want to have notifications, are being processed before the more generic conditions. Pay special attention to conditions that do not send notifications and those that close instances. If they are too high up in the sequence other conditions may always be ignored.

Processing order of conditions

Condition filters

Effective filters need to be of the same data format as the values in the CDS fields, such as strings or integers. You can find the reference to your object’s CDS views in the Manage Situation Objects app.

Sometimes the conditions and their filter values have been set up in such a way that no instances are created. Check whether one of the following reasons has prevented the creation of a situation instance:

  • The condition attribute filters are too constrained for your use case.
  • The attribute filter values overlap.
  • The attribute filter values are contradictory.

The condition and filter settings depend on your use case and the situation template on which your situation type is based. If you use multiple conditions, you can easily compare filters using the Compare Filters function.

Compare condition filters

Compare condition filters used

You can find some examples of using multiple conditions with filters in the blog posts Custom Situation Cases: Configure an Escalation Case and Custom Situation Cases: Configure a Complex Case.

 

Are all situation triggers covered?

Complex use cases often require multiple situation triggers. Check whether you have configured all possible triggers. And ensure that all triggers are enabled. There are two kinds of triggers: batch-based and event-based triggers.

Batch-based triggers periodically check the data you have defined in the condition filters.

  • The triggering object can be the anchor object or the trigger object.
  • The behavior of the trigger object is always Add/Update because the object still exists.

Use the Trigger Batch function to test immediately whether the situation trigger works as expected. Also check batch scheduling to make sure it’s configured correctly.

Batch-based situation triggers

Event-based triggers are based on an object’s status update. You can use multiple event-based triggers where you need to consider three aspects:

  • The triggering object: Multiple objects may impact a situation.
  • The trigger events such as create, update, and remove: Multiple events may impact a situation for a specific object.
  • The behavior of the trigger object can be Add/Update, Remove (Trigger data available), or Remove (Trigger data not available)

Event-based situation triggers

And these three aspects need to go hand in hand.

Situation objects that are created always have the trigger object behavior Add/Update because the object is added and exists.

The events that change an object can be manifold, for example, updated, confirmed, rejected, in progress, for approval, completed, released, posted, published, started, activated, blocked, unlocked, and many more. If the object still exists after the change, the trigger object behavior is Add/Update. If the object is flagged as removed or deleted, the trigger object behavior is Remove (Trigger data available). 

Objects that are deleted always have the trigger object behavior Remove (trigger data not available). This means the object is physically gone along with all its data. No more references are possible.

You can find an example for multiple triggers in the blog post Custom Situation Cases: Configure a Complex Case.

Should a situation trigger be missing in the situation type, you have the following options in the test system:

  1. Add a situation trigger from the value help in the situation type.
  2. If no appropriate situation trigger is available for the situation type, you can add a situation trigger with the suitable event to your situation scenario (Manage Situation Scenarios app). To do this you need to have the extensibility specialist role. Then proceed with (1).  Optionally, you can add the situation trigger to the related template so that it’s automatically available for all situation types created from the situation template.
  3. If no matching situation trigger is available in the situation scenario, you can enhance or add a situation object with the missing situation trigger (Manage Situation Objects app). Again, you need the extensibility specialist role. Then proceed with (2) and (1).

Afterwards, transport your situation type, situation scenario, and situation object to the productive system.

 

Is recipient determination working?

Situation Handling uses Responsibility Management to determine the users that are responsible for a situation. In the My Situations – Extended app, users see the created situations, and, depending on the notification settings, receive notifications.

When you create a situation type in the Recipients section, there are two basic options for defining these users:

  • Responsibility by Teams, which determines the users with the help of a team category.
  • Responsibility Rules, which determines the users with a BAdI business logic.

Situation recipients

Responsibility by Teams

To determine the right users by teams, a team category needs to be in place. The team category links Situation Handling and Responsibility Management. For a team category, at least one team needs to be enabled so that the right users can be detected. You enable teams in the Manage Teams and Responsibilities app. Also check whether all enabled teams have team members assigned to them and include the users who have to be informed about the situation.

Situation recipients by teams

You can add team members by selecting users from the value help. If you can’t find the user in the value help, check whether this user is registered as a business partner. If the business partner registration is missing, contact your system administrator to have your users registered.

If you use filters for responsibility definition or member functions in your situation type, make sure they are appropriate and don’t exclude your users. For detailed information about these filters, see the blog post Using Responsibility Teams in Situation Handling.

Responsibility Rules

Responsibility rules use the logic of a BAdI to determine the right users. If the identification of users isn’t working as expected, the logic of the BAdI can be the reason why your users were not found and therefore did not get any notifications.

Situation recipients by rules

If your situation type uses a standard responsibility rule, please open a ticket on component CA-GTF-RM to have the rule checked.

For your custom implementation, you can check the BAdI implementation logic yourself. (You’ll need the SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC role to do that). Then proceed as follows:

  • In the Manage Responsibility Context app, on the Extended tab, search for the parent responsibility context to find your extended responsibility context.
  • Open the extended responsibility context to display the details. Here, you can see the responsibility rule ID associated with the responsibility rule that is displayed in the situation type.
  • In the Custom Logic app, you need to filter the list for the responsibility rule business context. Then, search for the static filter value that is identical to the responsibility rule ID. In the details, you can then check the BAdI implementation logic.

For detailed information about custom responsibility rules, you can refer to the blog post Responsibility Management: Create Your Rules. The blog post Using Responsibility Rules in Situation Handling illustrates how responsibility rules and Situation Handling work hand in hand.

 

Have email notifications been set up correctly?

In addition to the SAP Fiori launchpad notifications, users can opt to receive email notifications for each situation type in their SAP Fiori launchpad settings. For details, see Enable Email Notifications for End Users.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 email notifications are enabled automatically. In older releases users need to enable email notifications in their SAP Fiori launchpad settings. To be able to activate this option, users need to have received an SAP Fiori launchpad notification before they can see and select the situation type in the launchpad settings.

Notification settings

Users may also want to check the launchpad settings to see whether the email address for their user account is correct:

Email settings

If all the settings are correct and you're still not getting any emails, you may want to check whether the email domain is on the list of allowed domains. The list of sender domains is maintained in Output Management. You can find more information in the chapter Defining an Email Sender Domain in the Setup Guide for SAP Best Practices for Output Management.

For specific questions or issues with the notification framework, please open a ticket on component OPU-GW-NOT.

 

Additional prerequisites for SAP S/4HANA

As an SAP S/4HANA customer, you may need to check some additional settings for the Notification Channel Framework which are automatically in place for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers. You can also refer to the blog post Enable Email Notification in Notification Channel Framework. If you want to enable email notifications per default, you can implement the SAP Note 3417113.

To be on the safe side, you may also want to check whether you have made the required technical configuration settings.

  • Set up technical jobs
  • Activate OData services for apps
  • Define authorization for apps (optional)
  • Activate OData services for APIs (optional)
  • Define authorizations for APIs (optional)

 

Did this help you?

If not → just open a ticket on our component CA-SIT-ENG and we’ll take care of your problem. In the ticket, please let us know the situation scenario and the situation template your situation type is based on. Please attach screenshots of your situation type’s Conditions and Recipients sections. Or tell us which of the steps above you’ve already tried.

 

For more information, see

SAP Help Portal for

  • Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA.

SAP Community

  • Intelligent Situation Handling

entry.title: Search, Select, and Straighten
entry.content:
Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC) offers a wide variety of tools for managing configurable products. Characteristics and their values, describing the properties of a configurable material, make up the scaffold of configuration models – including a more or less complex net of dependencies between the characteristics and characteristic values.

Sometimes, the number of characteristics in a model can reach an enormous level, and you can quickly lose track of things: Which group does the characteristic belong to? Where in the BOM hierarchy is it located? It would be nice if you could search for characteristics in a vast model effectively, and even better if you could also directly valuate the characteristic you’ve been searching for. As you might have guessed, we’ve got good news: Searching and working within search results is now possible (since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408).

This is how it works: On the valuation screen in the simulation, there’s a new search field.

Here, you can enter the technical name or description of a characteristic. Whether you can use the technical name or the description (or both) depends on the settings for the description mode:

You can adjust this setting to your ease.

The search is case-insensitive and, important to know, it comprises all characteristic groups and configurable components of the bill of materials (BOM). This way, you’re not limited to the selected characteristic group on the screen.

As you can see in the following example, searching for "length" returns results from different BOM positions in the model:

You can immediately assign (or remove) values to the displayed characteristics when you see the results list.

This new feature will make handling the characteristic values of even humongous models much easier!

Check this link to our documentation to watch a short video showing the search feature: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/11d7452297a74a499d56f5049daa3606.html?version=2408.500.

entry.title: Customer Course GW100 (SAP Gateway – Building OData Services) Available for SAP S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
The GW100 got a “technical update”, which means that all exercises are now running on the newest SAP S/4HANA available today. In addition, the slides now follow the newest design guidelines of SAP. So, welcome to this short blog post about the customer training GW100 (SAP Gateway – Building OData Services).

If you are more interested in a digital learning for SAP Gateway, please read my blog post:
SAP Learning Journey – Building OData Services with SAP Gateway

Introduction

Starting in 2014, this is now the eighth incarnation of how to develop OData services with SAP Gateway. The course shows all parts of SAP Gateway, which are available since SAP NetWeaver Application Server 7.0 (SAP Gateway 2.0), as well as parts added up to SAP S/4HANA 2023. The whole bandwidth of SAP Gateway covering all releases is shown.

The units in the current version are the following:

  1. SAP Gateway Overview
  2. OData Overview
  3. SAP Gateway Service Implementation
  4. SAP Gateway Service Generation
  5. SAP Gateway Service Redefinition
  6. SAP Gateway and CDS Views
  7. SAP Gateway Hub Functionalities
  8. Advanced OData Options
  9. SAP Gateway Security
  10. Further Information

If customers are interested, they can order a customer specific version of the course including only those topics suitable for their release or purpose.

System Landscape

Let’s start with the system release: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02. Our system landscape – we call it universal target – is used in many technology courses and offers full access for the participants from the SAP Fiori launchpad (FLP) in the browser down to SAP HANA on the SUSE Linux Enterprise server (SLES). Everything is set up following the newest guidelines of SAP so that it can really act as a template for customers.

GW100 System Landscape (Screenshot from System Setup Guide)

In addition, we offer an instance of the SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) to show the consumption of OData services. If you want to know more about this topic, check out our courses for SAP Fiori development.

Example: Business Services

As an example from the material, here a shorten excerpt about defining business services.

Business Services: OData V2 and V4 (Screenshot from GW100)

In the area of business services, a service definition exposes CDS Views as a generic service. A service binding then marks it as a special kind of service. You can create an OData V2 or V4 service for API- or UI-usage. Service definitions and bindings are created in the ADT, the generated OData services are registered in transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE or /IWFND/V4_ADMIN respectively.

How to Create a Service Definition and Binding (Screenshot from GW100)

Service definitions and bindings are ABAP repository objects and a substantial part of the business services. They can only be created and edited in the ADT. In the first step, the service definition just defines the name of the service. The CDS views are added later in the source code.

In the second step, the service binding refers to the service definition and sets the binding type, which are mainly communication protocols.

Service Definition and Binding Correlation (Screenshot from GW100)

By exposing a CDS View in a service definition, the name of the entity set can be defined. For the entity type, "Type" is automatically appended to the entity set name.

The entity set names and navigation properties based on the associations in the CDS views are visible in the service binding on the right. For OData V2, multiple versions of a service definition can be added to a service binding visible on the left. For OData V4, multiple service definitions can be added to one service binding to allow cross-service navigation.


Summary

If you have read so far, it seems that you are really thinking about attending the course. Don’t hesitate and check out the physical and virtual events we offer:

https://training.sap.com/course/GW100

If you are more interested in a digital learning, please visit https://learning.sap.com. Some parts of the GW100 are already available free-of-charge:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/building-odata-services-with-sap-gateway

For any other questions around the offerings of SAP Learning Services, please jump to our community page and get in contact with us:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/training-certification

I hope even if you won’t attend the GW100 in any kind, you found some useful information around SAP Gateway in this blog post.

Happy learning
Stefan

entry.title: Where can I start learning how to utilize the SAP Self-Billing Cockpit within SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Implementing SAP Self-Billing Cockpit learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will focus on the SAP Self-Billing Cockpit, an SAP Industry Cloud solution designed to streamline and automate self-billing processes. The key functionalities such as receiving self-billing transmissions, updating invoices and journal entries, detecting and recording differences, and preparing documents for the clearing process in accounts receivable will be covered. You will learn to analyze and monitor usage, manage error lists, and understand the commercial model and licensing metrics. The course aims to equip SAP consultants with the knowledge to effectively implement and utilize the SAP Self-Billing Cockpit within SAP S/4HANA environments.

Learning objectives
At the end of this learning journey, participants will be able to effectively navigate and utilize the SAP Self-Billing Cockpit within SAP S/4HANA environments. Learners will gain proficiency in receiving self-billing transmissions, updating invoices and journal entries, and managing the clearing process in accounts receivable. Additionally, the course will cover how to detect and record discrepancies in self-billing documents and prepare them for confirmation. Participants will also learn to analyze and monitor usage, manage error lists, and understand the commercial model and licensing metrics associated with the SAP Self-Billing Cockpit. This comprehensive understanding will enable SAP consultants to implement and optimize the use of the SAP Self-Billing Cockpit, enhancing their ability to streamline and automate self-billing processes effectively.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Changes in the Communication of the Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy
entry.content:
Versão em português

Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil team has released updates on the communication of the Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy (NF3-e) solution with SEFAZ.

NF3-e Technical Note 2024.002 1.01 Version

This update complies with the SEFAZ NF3-e Technical Note 2024.002 1.01 Version, which announced the discontinuation of the asynchronous authorization service, also known as the batch submission service. The communication between the SAP service and SEFAZ now is synchronous.

This communication change has been updated in the outbound invoicing option for Brazil service, and it is not necessary to implement SAP Notes in your backend system. For official communication on this update, read the SAP Note 3509349.

Check out the latest updates and features in our What's New session. And for a step-by-step guide on how to use the Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy (NF3-e) solution, refer to the following SAP Help Portal pages:

  • Integration > NF3-e Specifics
  • Using Outbound Nota Fiscal > NF3-e Specifics

Related blog posts:

  • Usage Analytics Dashboard
  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2478289.

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR-OU (Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy, NF3-e)
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT (Nota Fiscal Eletrônica Outbound)

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “like” (kudos), share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content. You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance community to keep up with the latest NF-e news.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago

entry.title: Mudanças na comunicação da nota fiscal eletrônica de energia elétrica
entry.content:
English version here

Olá pessoal,

O time de SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de saída para o Brasil entregou atualizações na comunicação da solução de nota fiscal eletrônica de energia elétrica (NF3-e) com a SEFAZ.

NF3-e Nota Técnica 2024.002 Versão 1.01

 


Esta atualização está conforme a Nota Técnica 2024.002 versão 1.01 de NF3-e da SEFAZ, que anunciou a descontinuação do serviço de autorização assíncrona, também conhecido como serviço de envio em lote. A comunicação entre o serviço da SAP e a SEFAZ agora é síncrona.

Esta alteração de comunicação foi atualizada na opção de emissão de fatura de saída para o Brasil, e não é necessário implementar o SAP Notes no seu sistema backend. Para comunicação oficial sobre esta atualização, leia a SAP Note 3509349.

Confira as últimas atualizações e recursos na página Novidades do Nota fiscal de saída no portal SAP Help. Para obter um passo a passo sobre como usar a solução de nota fiscal eletrônica de energia elétrica (NF3-e), consulte as seguintes páginas do portal SAP Help:

  • Integração > Específicos da NF3-e
  • Utilizar a nota fiscal de saída > Específicos da NF3-e

Blog posts relacionados:

  • Dashboard do Usage Analytics
  • Como navegar facilmente nas páginas do SAP Help Portal para Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • Navegando no portal SAP Help (inglês)

Você pode receber alerta por email quando novas SAP Notes forem lançadas ou atualizadas nos componentes listados abaixo; basta seguir as instruções do SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR-OU (Nota fiscal eletrônica de energia elétrica, NF3-e)
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT (Nota Fiscal Eletrônica Outbound)

Acesse o gerenciador de modificações legais para ter uma noção do que está planejado para ser suportado. Consulte o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 e a página Regulatory Change Manager para entender como usar esta ferramenta.

Gostou desse post? Dê um “like” (kudos) e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. Sinta-se à vontade para deixar suas perguntas no SAP Community e ler posts semelhantes no blogging space do SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. Para acompanhar as últimas notícias da NF-e, siga a tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) e a comunidade SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Obrigado e até a próxima,
Thiago

entry.title: Recent Updates on Manifesto Eletrônico de Documentos Fiscais
entry.content:
Versão em português

Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil team has released updates on the Manifesto Eletrônico de Documentos Fiscais (MDF-e). The update complies with SEFAZ Technical Note 2024.002 1.01 Version. 

The new update changes the layout of the XML files, which now have the mapping of the following fields: indPrestacaoParcial, infNFePresParcial, and chNFe. You can implement these changes in the outbound invoicing option for Brazil by following the instructions on the SAP Note 3508360.

Take a look at the latest updates and features in our What's New session. And for a step-by-step guide on how to enable your system to submit MDF-e (Manifesto Eletrônico de Documentos Fiscais), refer to the following SAP Help Portal pages:

  • Integration > MDF-e Specifics
  • Using Outbound Nota Fiscal > MDF-e Specifics

Related blog posts:

  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT
  • LOD-LH-NFE

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “like” (kudos), share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content.

You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance community to keep up with the latest NF-e news.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago

entry.title: MDF-e Nota Técnica 2024.002 Versão 1.01: Novos campos no Manifesto Eletrônico de Documentos Fiscais
entry.content:
English version here

Olá pessoal,

O time de SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de saída para o Brasil entregou atualizações no Manifesto Eletrônico de Documentos Fiscais (MDF-e). A atualização está conforme a Nota Técnica 2024.002 Versão 1.01 da SEFAZ.

A nova atualização altera o layout dos arquivos XML, que agora têm o mapeamento dos seguintes campos: indPrestacaoParcial, infNFePresParcial e chNFe. Você pode implementar essas alterações na opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil seguindo as instruções da SAP Note 3508360.

Confira as últimas atualizações e recursos na página Novidades do Nota fiscal de saída no portal SAP Help. E para um guia passo a passo sobre como habilitar seu sistema para enviar MDF-e (Manifesto Eletrônico de Documentos Fiscais), consulte as seguintes páginas do portal SAP Help:

  • Integração > Específicos de MDF-e
  • Utilizar o Nota fiscal de saída > Específicos de MDF-e

Blog posts relacionados:

  • Dashboard do Usage Analytics
  • Como navegar facilmente nas páginas do SAP Help Portal para Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • Navegando no portal SAP Help (inglês)

Você pode receber alerta por email quando novas SAP Notes forem lançadas ou atualizadas nos componentes listados abaixo; basta seguir as instruções do SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT
  • LOD-LH-NFE

Acesse o gerenciador de modificações legais para ter uma noção do que está planejado para ser suportado. Consulte o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 e a página Regulatory Change Manager para entender como usar esta ferramenta.

Gostou desse post? Dê um “like” (kudos) e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. Sinta-se à vontade para deixar suas perguntas no SAP Community e ler posts semelhantes no blogging space do SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. Para acompanhar as últimas notícias da NF-e, siga a tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) e a comunidade SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Obrigado e até a próxima,
Thiago

Tags: brazil, brasil, einvoice, einvoicing, e-invoicing, electronic invoice, electronic invoicing, edocument, edoc, e-document, e-docs, e-doc, e-docs, drc, document compliance, nota fiscal eletrônica, electronic nota fiscal, outbound, nf3-e, nf-e, electric energy, energia elétrica, is-u, nota técnica 2024.002, 2024/002, 2024.00

entry.title: Creating ODATA Draft Scenario for CURD Operations
entry.content:
ODATA DRAFT SCENARIOS 

 What is Draft? 

A draft is the state of the business object when its data is not persisted in the actual tables but is stored in some temporary tables which can be quickly retrieved to continue working (from the place left) in case the work is left in the middle due to some reason. Draft Management Process Flow: 

Consider an application with Sales Order (SO) Header and Item data. The data can be displayed and updated or new SOs can be created. 

Drafts play no role in Display functionality. It is when any user either enters into an update mode or a create mode drafts are involved. 

A Draft can be: 

  • Created when the user enters into update mode – all actual table data is copied for temporary tables/draft tables and then data is presented on the screen for the user to update. 
  • Created when the user enters into create mode- table entries with draft keys are created in draft tables. 
  • Updated when the user enters data in any field, changes a tab on the screen, on any clicks, the press of a button, or at a set time depending on the requirement of the application. Any one of the trigger points or a combination of these trigger points can be used to implement this part. 
  • Deleted when a draft is discarded by the user, or when the data is saved to actual tables. 

Implement Draft Management: 

Create Draft tables to store the data temporarily. In this case, draft tables will be require for Header and Item data. 

Tables for draft data: 

Steps to create Entity types: 

Go to SAP Gateway Service Builder(SEGW). 

 

Click on create project node. 

 

Provide the project name, description, package name and click on Continue. 

 

Select the Data model and Right click on data model. 

 

Select Import and click on DDIC Structures node. 

Provide the entity name and DDIC table name which table you want to import. 

Click on Next. 

Select the required fields which you want to display/perform operations. 

Click on Next. 

 

Select the atleast one key fields from that structure and click on finish. 

Create one more entity type for Item details. 

Click on Save. 

 

Click on Generate Runtime Objects node. 

 

By default/Implicitly 4 classes, one technical model and one technical service is created. 

Click on continue. 

In addition to this one more entity with just a text field is defined to be used as the return type 

Create one return type structure. 

Add one return type. 

Steps to create Association : 

Right click on Data model. 

 

Select Create and click on Association node. 

Provide the Association name, Principle name as Header entity name, Dependent name as Item entity name and provide the cardinality for displaying the item details and provide the navigation property name. 

Click on Next. 

Provide dependent property name as common field name for both header and item entity. 

Click on next. 

Click on Finish. 

Create one deep structure in MPC_EXT class. 

Provide Navigation property name for item details like as shown in below 

 

Steps to create Function Import: 

3 function imports, CreateDraft, DeleteDraft, and SaveOrder are created to achieve the functionality. All use the Return Type as the entity type Return. 

Right click on Data model. 

 

Select the create and Click on Function import. 

Provide the Function import name, Click on continue. 

Provide the return type kind as Entity type, Return type as Return entity name, pass the cardinality and select the HTTP method as post. 

Like this create 2 more function import for DeleteDraft, and SaveOrder. 

Provide the parameter for function import. 

Code Reference from ZCL_ZGR_ODATA_DRAFT_SC_DPC_EXT 

 

Read and Query Operations: 

These operations usually will refer to the actual tables/Standard BAPI etc., to get data.

 

 

method SO_HEADERSET_GET_ENTITY. 
  
    DATA : lv_vbeln TYPE vbak-vbeln. 
  
    DATA(it_keys) = io_tech_request_context->get_keys( ). 
  
    lv_vbeln = VALUE #( it_keys[ name = 'VBELN' ]-value optional ). 
     lv_vbeln = |{ lv_vbeln ALPHA = IN }|. 
  
    select SINGLE  FROM vbak 
                   FIELDS vbeln, 
                          erdat, 
                          erzet, 
                          ernam, 
                          netwr, 
                          waerk, 
                          vkorg, 
                          vtweg, 
                          spart, 
                          kunnr 
                     WHERE vbeln = _vbeln 
                   INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF _entity. 
  
  endmethod.

 

 

 

 

method SO_HEADERSET_GET_ENTITYSET. 
  
    SELECT FROM vbak 
     FIELDS vbeln, erdat, erzet, ernam, netwr, 
            waerk, vkorg, vtweg, spart, kunnr 
     INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE _entityset 
     UP TO 20 ROWS. 
  
  endmethod.

 

 

Read/ Get Item data: 

 

 

method SO_ITEMSET_GET_ENTITY. 
  
    DATA ls_keys TYPE zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc=>ts_so_item. 
  
    io_tech_request_context->get_converted_keys( 
       IMPORTING 
         es_key_values = ls_keys                 " Entity Key Values - converted 
     ). 
  
     SELECT SINGLE FROM vbap 
                    FIELDS vbeln, posnr, matnr, arktx, zmeng, meins, 
                           netwr, waerk, werks, lgort, vstel, route 
                     WHERE vbeln = _keys-vbeln 
                           AND   posnr = _keys-posnr 
                     INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF _entity. 
   endmethod.

 

 

 

 

METHOD so_itemset_get_entityset. 
  
    DATA lv_vbeln TYPE vbak-vbeln. 
  
    IF io_tech_request_context->get_source_entity_type_name( ) = 'SO_Header'. 
  
      DATA(It_Keys) = io_tech_request_context->get_source_keys( ). 
  
      lv_vbeln = VALUE #( it_keys[ name = 'Vbeln' ]-value OPTIONAL ). 
       lv_vbeln = |{ lv_vbeln ALPHA = IN }|. 
  
      SELECT FROM vbap 
       FIELDS vbeln, posnr, matnr, arktx, zmeng, meins, 
          netwr, waerk, werks, lgort, vstel, route 
       WHERE vbeln = _vbeln 
       INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE _entityset. 
  
    ELSE. 
  
      SELECT FROM vbap 
              FIELDS vbeln, posnr, matnr, arktx, zmeng, meins, 
                     netwr, waerk, werks, lgort, vstel, route 
               INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE _entityset. 
  
    ENDIF. 
  
  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

CREATE_DRAFT Method: 

An additional method is created in the DPC_EXT class with importing parameter VBELN i.e., Order Number and Exporting parameter Return which is the same type as extra entity. 

This method will check weather the draft exists for the order. If it does and it is by another user, it will return an error text. Otherwise, the method will copy the data from the standard table to custom draft tables ZGR_T_SOH_DRAFT and ZGR_T_SOI_DRAFT. 

To Keep the logic simple, the method uses incremental logic to generate a key. In an actual scenario, we will either use a hash generator or number range to create the draft key. 

Note: Create_Draft method is a custom method which is have importing and Returning parameter. 

 

 

  method CREATE_DRAFT. 
  
    "Check if draft exists 
     SELECT SINGLE FROM zgr_t_soh_draft 
                   FIELDS * 
                   INTO (ls_draft). 
  
      IF sy-subrc EQ 0. "Draft exist 
  
        IF sy-uname = ls_draft-ernam. "Draft by same user 
  
          RETURN = VALUE zgr_s_return( test = 'S Draft success' ). 
  
          ELSE. 
  
            RETURN-test = |E Draft user : { ls_draft-ernam }|. 
  
        ENDIF. 
  
      ELSE. "No Draft 
  
        SELECT SINGLE FROM vbak 
                       FIELDS vbeln, erdat, erzet, ernam, netwr, 
                              waerk, vkorg, vtweg, spart, kunnr 
                       WHERE vbeln = @vbeln 
                       INTO (ls_vbak). 
  
          IF sy-subrc EQ 0. 
  
            ls_draft = CORRESPONDING #( ls_vbak ). 
             ls_draft-ernam = sy-uname. 
  
            INSERT zgr_t_soh_draft FROM ls_draft. 
  
            IF sy-subrc EQ 0. 
  
              RETURN-test = 'S Draft Success'. 
  
              ELSE. 
                 RETURN-test = 'S Draft Failed'. 
  
 
            ENDIF. 
  
          ENDIF. 
  
          "Add items to the draft 
  
          SELECT  FROM vbap 
                  FIELDS mandt, vbeln, posnr, matnr, arktx, zmeng, meins, 
                         netwr, waerk, werks, lgort, vstel, route 
                  WHERE vbeln = @vbeln 
                  INTO TABLE (lt_items). 
  
            IF sy-subrc EQ 0. 
  
              INSERT zgr_t_soi_draft FROM TABLE _items. 
  
            ENDIF. 
  
      ENDIF. 
  
  endmethod.

 

 

CREATE Operation: 

Create Operation should be called only for item data. For header data instead of create, created draft should be called. Item create operation, actually only creates an entry in the draft table. 

 

 

method SO_ITEMSET_CREATE_ENTITY. 
  
  DATA:  ls_Final TYPE zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc_ext=>TY_FINAL, 
          ls_item type zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc=>ts_so_item. 
  
  io_data_provider->read_entry_data( 
     IMPORTING 
       es_data = ls_final 
   ). 
  
  ls_item = CORRESPONDING #( ls_final ). 
  
  INSERT zgr_t_soi_draft FROM ls_item. 
  
  IF Sy-subrc EQ 0. 
  
    er_entity = ls_item. 
  
  ENDIF. 
  
  endmethod.

 

 

UPDATE Operation: 

Update operation for header and item entities will update the draft tables. 

Update Operation For Header Data:

 

 

  method SO_HEADERSET_UPDATE_ENTITY. 
  
    DATA: ls_final TYPE zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc_ext=>ty_final, 
           ls_header TYPE zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc_ext=>ts_so_header. 
  
    io_data_provider->read_entry_data( 
       IMPORTING 
         es_data = ls_final 
     ). 
  
    ls_header = CORRESPONDING #( ls_final ). 
  
    IF sy-subrc Eq 0. 
  
      MODIFY zgr_t_soh_draft FROM ls_header. 
  
        er_entity = ls_header. 
  
    ENDIF. 
  
  endmethod.

 

 

Update operation for Item data: 

 

 

 method SO_ITEMSET_UPDATE_ENTITY. 
  
    DATA: ls_final TYPE zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc_ext=>ty_final, 
           ls_item TYPE zcl_zgr_odata_draft_sc_mpc_ext=>ts_so_item. 
  
    io_data_provider->read_entry_data( 
       IMPORTING 
         es_data = ls_final 
     ). 
  
    ls_item = CORRESPONDING #( ls_final ). 
  
    IF sy-subrc EQ 0. 
  
      MODIFY zgr_t_soi_draft FROM ls_item. 
  
    ENDIF. 
  
  endmethod.

 

 

DELETE Operation: 

In this case, a delete operation is not implemented as a Sales Order can not be deleted from the system. Usually, it will be set as all items rejected etc., which can be done in an update operation. 

Function Import Implementation: 

Method /IWBEP/IF_MGW_APPL_SRV_RUNTIME~EXECUTE_ACTION is redefined to implement the function imports. 

CreateDraft: 

  • Call the method Create_draft with the Sales order number. 

DeleteDraft: 

  • Delete the draft data from the header and item draft tables. 

SaveOrder: 

  • Check if the order number exists. If it does, it is a change mode, else it is a create mode. 
  • Get data from draft tables. 
  • Call create/change BAPI. 
  • Return the Message with order number. 

 

 

Function Import implementation:

 

 

  METHOD /iwbep/if_mgw_appl_srv_runtime~execute_action. 
  
    DATA : lv_vbeln_out TYPE vbeln. 
     DATA : ls_header_in TYPE bapisdhd1, 
            lt_items_in  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapisditm, 
            lt_partners  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiparnr, 
            lt_return    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiret2. 
  
    CASE iv_action_name. 
       WHEN 'CreateDraft'. 
  
        TRY. 
  
          DATA(lt_pars) = io_tech_request_context->get_parameters( ). 
           DATA(lv_vbeln) = VALUE vbeln_va( lt_pars[ name = 'SALESORDER' ]-value OPTIONAL ). 
  
          lv_vbeln = |{ lv_vbeln ALPHA = IN }|. 
           DATA(ls_return) = create_draft( vbeln = lv_vbeln  ). 
  
        CATCH cx_sy_itab_line_not_found. 
  
          "Error 
  
          ls_return-test = 'E Invalid call'. 
  
        ENDTRY. 
  
        copy_data_to_ref( 
           EXPORTING 
             is_data = ls_return 
           CHANGING 
             cr_data = er_data 
         ). 
  
      WHEN 'DeleteDraft'. 
          
         TRY. 
           lt_pars = io_tech_request_context->get_parameters( ). 
           lv_vbeln = VALUE vbeln_va( lt_pars[ name = 'SALESORDER' ]-value ). 
           lv_vbeln = |{ lv_vbeln ALPHA = IN }|. 
           DELETE FROM zgr_t_soh_draft WHERE vbeln = _vbeln. 
           IF sy-subrc EQ 0. 
  
            DELETE FROM zgr_t_soi_draft WHERE vbeln = _vbeln. 
  
            ls_return-test = 'S draft deleted'. 
  
          ENDIF. 
  
        CATCH cx_sy_itab_line_not_found. 
           "Error 
           ls_return-test = 'E Invalid call'. 
  
        ENDTRY. 
         copy_data_to_ref( 
           EXPORTING 
             is_data = ls_return 
           CHANGING 
             cr_data = er_data 
         ). 
  
      WHEN 'SaveOrder'. 
  
        TRY. 
  
          lt_pars = io_tech_request_context->get_parameters( ). 
           lv_vbeln = VALUE vbeln_va( lt_pars[ name = 'SALESORDER' ]-value OPTIONAL ). 
           lv_vbeln = |{ lv_vbeln ALPHA = IN }|. 
  
          select SINGLE vbeln FROM vbak into (lv_vbeln_Test) 
                   WHERE vbeln = _vbeln. 
  
            IF sy-subrc NE 0. 
  
              CLEAR lv_vbeln_test. 
  
            ENDIF. 
  
            SELECT SINGLE FROM zgr_t_soh_draft 
                       FIELDS * 
                       WHERE vbeln = _vbeln 
                       INTO (ls_header). 
  
              IF sy-subrc NE 0. 
  
                ls_return-test = 'E Sales order not found'. 
  
                ELSE. 
  
                  "Fetch draft items 
  
                  SELECT FROM zgr_t_soi_draft 
                          FIELDS * 
                          WHERE vbeln = _vbeln 
                          INTO TABLE (lt_item). 
  
                    IF lv_vbeln_test IS NOT INITIAL. "Change mode 
  
                      DATA : ls_headerx_in TYPE bapisdh1x, 
                              ls_uheader_in TYPE bapisdhd1, 
                              lt_itemsx_in TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapisditmx. 
  
                      lt_items_in = VALUE #( FOR  ls_item IN lt_item 
                                           ( itm_number = ls_item-posnr 
                                             short_text = ls_item-arktx 
                                            ) 
                                           ). 
  
 
                      lt_itemsx_in = VALUE #( FOR ls_item IN lt_item 
                                            ( itm_number = ls_item-posnr 
                                              updateflag = 'U' 
                                              short_text = 'X' 
                                            ) 
                                           ). 
  
                      ls_headerx_in-updateflag = 'U'. 
                       lv_vbeln_out = lv_vbeln_test. 
  
                      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE' 
                         EXPORTING 
                           salesdocument               = lv_vbeln_test 
                           order_header_inx            = ls_headerx_in 
                         TABLES 
                           return                      = lt_return 
                           ORDER_ITEM_IN               = lt_items_in 
                           ORDER_ITEM_INX              = lt_itemsx_in 
                                 . 
                      ELSE. 
  
                       ls_header_in-doc_type = 'ZOR1'. 
                        ls_header_in-doc_date = ls_header-erdat. 
                        ls_header_in-created_by = ls_header-ernam. 
                        ls_header_in-currency = ls_header-waerk. 
                        ls_header_in-sales_org = ls_header-vkorg. 
                        ls_header_in-distr_chan = ls_header-vtweg. 
                        ls_header_in-division = ls_header-spart. 
  
                       lt_partners = VALUE #( ( partn_role = 'AG' 
                                                 partn_numb = ls_header-kunnr ) ). 
                        lt_items_in = VALUE #( FOR LS_ITEM IN lt_item 
                                            ( itm_number = ls_item-posnr 
                                              material = ls_item-matnr 
                                              short_text = ls_item-arktx 
                                              target_qty = ls_item-zmeng 
                                              target_qu = ls_item-meins 
                                              plant = ls_item-werks 
                                              store_loc = ls_item-lgort 
                                              ship_point = ls_item-vstel 
                                              route = ls_item-route 
                                             ) 
                                            ). 
  
                       CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2' 
                          EXPORTING 
                            order_header_in               = ls_header_in 
                         IMPORTING 
                           SALESDOCUMENT                 = lv_vbeln_out 
                          tables 
                           RETURN                        = lt_return 
                           ORDER_ITEMS_IN                = lt_items_in 
                           order_partners                = lt_partners 
                                  . 
                     ENDIF. 
  
                    IF NOT line_exists( lt_return[ type = 'E' ] ). 
  
                      "Update FM to delete the draft 
                       CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT' 
 *                       EXPORTING 
 *                         WAIT          = 
 *                       IMPORTING 
 *                         RETURN        = 
                                 . 
  
                      "Delete Draft 
  
                      delete FROM zgr_t_soh_draft WHERE vbeln = lv_vbeln. 
                       delete FROM zgr_t_soi_draft WHERE vbeln = lv_vbeln. 
                       ls_return-test = lv_vbeln_out. 
  
                    ELSE. 
  
                      ls_return-test = 'E Error in Bapi Call'. 
  
                    ENDIF. 
  
              ENDIF. 
  
 
        CATCH cx_sy_itab_line_not_found. 
  
          "Error 
           ls_return-test = 'E Invalida call'. 
  
        ENDTRY. 
  
        me->copy_data_to_ref( 
           EXPORTING 
             is_data = ls_return 
           CHANGING 
             cr_data = er_data 
         ). 
     ENDCASE. 
   ENDMETHOD.

 

 

Steps to Register the Services: 

Goto SAP Gateway services (/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICES). 

 

Click on Add Services node. 

 

Provide the System Alias and Technical service name which is generated while generating the OData services. 

Click on Get services node. 

 

Select the services and click on Add Selected Services. 

 

Provide the Package name and click on continue. 

Select the services by using Filter. 

Click on SAP Gateway Client. 

Some basic scenarios output:

entry.title: PDM Newsletter RISE with SAP - October 2024
entry.content:
Welcome to the PDM Newsletter RISE with SAP Blog!

We're excited to introduce you to our monthly newsletter, your go-to resource for staying connected with the latest innovations, insights, and trends in the RISE with SAP ecosystem. Each edition is thoughtfully curated to provide exclusive expert guidance, inspiring stories, and actionable content designed to help you enhance your skills and stay ahead in the ever-evolving world of SAP.

Whether you're looking to explore emerging technologies or gain valuable knowledge tailored to your needs, our newsletter has you covered. We also encourage you to share this resource with your network and become an active member of our vibrant partner community.

Thank you for your continued support! Should you have any feedback or questions, don’t hesitate to reach out. We’re here to help you make the most of your RISE with SAP journey.

Stay tuned, stay informed, and sign up today! 

Let's grow together!

 

Victor Echeverri Reyes                                                               

 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Partner Delivery Manager                                                                                   Ecosystem Delivery Success   
 E: victor.echeverri.reyes@sap.com

 

 

 

Top Highlights

Overview                                     

  Register/Access

SAP Enterprise Cloud Services – Partner Summit 2024 (EMEA/MEE)

 

SAP Enterprise Cloud Services Partner Summit 2024' is a virtual summit to empower our SAP PartnerEdge, Sell and SAP PartnerEdge, Service partners. It will be scenario-based training comprised of some of the topics listed below.

 

            Register

 

  • Session: 15th October 2024
    09:00 AM to 12:00 PM CET

 

  • Session: 16th  October 2024
    09:00 AM to 11:30 PM CET

 

 

Rise into the future: Make your move to an AI-infused cloud ERP

On October 22 join SAP leaders, customers, partners, and industry experts virtually to learn about the real-world benefits of cloud ERP and AI. It’s time for customers to embrace the AI future so their businesses can innovate faster, execute efficiently, and stay agile.

           Register

 

  • Session: 22nd October 2024
    08:00 AM to 11:00 AM CEST

      

SAP TechEd Virtual

Join us for a high-energy, interactive virtual event. Gain skills and insights, learn about best practices and customer successes, and connect with tech experts, developers, and IT leaders for a rich learning and community-building experience

 

            Register

 

  • Session: 8-9th October 2024

 

      

Partner-Led Territories

Partner-Led Territories (PLT) was launched in 2023 as a key initiative to scale and accelerate high volume cloud growth in select territories with our most mature partners. In 2024, PLT will be further expanded as regions and market units are identifying more areas to be covered by this model based on the market potential and anticipated impact. Participating partners are expected to drive customer lifetime value with minimal support from SAP across the entire Customer Value Journey.

            Access

 

 

ABAP Cloud Partner Certification Academy

Expand your current skills with our new ABAP Cloud Partner Certification Academy. It will let you learn about and get ready to certification for ABAP Cloud faster than ever before.

.

 

             Register

 

  • Session: 22nd – 23rd – 24th – 29th October 2024
    08:00 AM to 12:30 PM Europe

      

RISE with SAP Methodology Advisory

This service offers advisory on RISE with SAP Methodology and clean core for partners embarking on customer engagements involving a transformation and transition to RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition via New Implementation or System Conversion.

             Access

SuccessConnect Virtual

 

SuccessConnect in 2024 will offer both an in-person and virtual experience. The official event dates are October 28–30, 2024. 

 

             Register

 

  • Session: 28th – 30th  October 2024

 

Events and Webinars for Partners

Overview

   Register/Access

Quarterly EMEA Partner Innovation Meeting Series

 

We are delighted to invite you to our next EMEA Partner Innovation Meeting on Wednesday 9th of October at 11 am CET. This will be our final chance to connect and discuss some important topics that will challenge, motivate and inspire us all to end 2024 on a high.

 

    Register

 

 

Partner enablement for seamless customer onboarding

In today’s competitive market, a smooth and successful implementation process is crucial for driving customer satisfaction and achieving long-term success. This session is designed to equip partners with the tools, knowledge, and best practices needed to guide customers through a seamless onboarding and implementation experience. Participants will learn how to anticipate and address common customer onboarding challenges. By empowering partners with the necessary tools and resources, we help ensure they are well-positioned to assist customers in achieving rapid success, building trust, and maximizing return on investment.

    Register

 

  • Session: 29th October 2024
    10:00 AM to 11:00 AM Americas

 

 

Deep insights on embedded launch activities for RISE with SAP customers

This session can provide the clear understanding of the onboarding process of RISE with SAP customers as well as the embedded launch activities process, documentations, resources and interlock with SAP Activate methodology

    Replay

Understand the SAP Business Accelerator Hub

The SAP Business Accelerator Hub is the place to discover business accelerator to speed up implementation but with nearly more than 34K individual artefacts, navigating the site can be daunting. I have seen it grow from a handful of packages to the what it is today, I would like to share my perspective on the website.

    Register

 

  • Session: 23rd October 2024
    08:00 AM to 09:00 AM Europe
  • Session: 23rd October 2024
    03:00 PM to 04:00 PM Europe

 

 

RISE with SAP add-on package for automotive

SAP is known for its depth and breadth of industry solutions. Since the early years of SAP ERP, SAP has enhanced the business capabilities provided by the core ERP with industry-specific capabilities that address the core business needs of companies in their respective industries.

 

  Register

 

  • Session: 18th September 2024
    03:30 PM to 04:30 PM CET

 

ERP-Centric EPM – transform how you face the future based on granular, reliable, instant insights

 

SAP’s ERP-Centric approach to SAP Enterprise Performance Management is designed the way it is to help companies move their business forward based on instant, multi-dimensional insights to maximize financial performance while optimize the tax and sustainability footprint at the same time. It takes out outdated silos of statutory accounting, management controlling and corporate tax management with one integrated approach based on unique transactional truth.

 

  Register

 

  • Session: 9th October 2024
    08:00 AM to 09:00 AM CET
  • Session: 9th October 2024
    11:00 AM to 12:00 PM EST

Expand the value of RISE with SAP with add-on packages for chief financial officer and chief supply chain officer

 

SAP highly values the commitment of our partner community for the success of our RISE with SAP strategy. Our customers need to understand the full value of our suite of private cloud solutions. For our partner community, it is important to position all SAP product bundles precisely while highlighting the benefits of SAP’s cloud-based AI-enabled suite of solutions

 

  Register

 

  • Session: 11th October 2024
    09:00 AM to 10:00 AM America

 

Rise into the future: Managed cloud services and security in RISE with SAP

 

RISE with SAP includes an AI-enabled cloud ERP that’s managed and optimized by SAP. A primary benefit for IT is to let SAP experts handle the day-to-day technical management of your cloud ERP infrastructure. Join us in a 30-minute webinar to hear RISE with SAP can help your IT team.

 

    Register

 

  • Session: 10th September 2024
    11:00 AM to 11:30 AM CEST

 

Masterclass for Customer Success on SAP’s cloud security and data protection

This session is designed to provide an overview of the tools and presentation material to confidently answer customer concerns around cloud security and customer data protection across SAP’s solution portfolio and instill trust in the customer’s cloud business transformation with SAP.

 

    Register

 

  • Session 1: 5th  September 2024
    09:00 AM to 09:45 AM CEST
  • Session 2: 5th  September 2024
    04:00 PM to 04:45 PM CEST

Enhancing Support Quality, Troubleshooting, and Collaboration with SAP

 

For a superior customer experience and to mitigate unnecessary delay and disruption, partners should use own users when engaging SAP support on customer behalf. Customers and partners are recommended to use the service partner user management application to authorize partner’s S-user ID(s) in SAP for Me on their behalf. Learn more about this application and excel in support-related aspects.

 

    Access

 

 

 

Business AI

Overview

Register/Access

Explore Generative AI Hub with SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad

SAP AI Core is a service in the SAP Business Technology Platform that helps customers and partners deploy and run AI models at scale without compromising data privacy and cost-effectiveness. It provides seamless integration with SAP solutions and any AI scenario can be easily realized using open-source frameworks

 

   Access

SAP AI Core

SAP AI Core helps you to develop and productize AI functions with out-of-the-box integration with SAP solutions.

   Access

 

Generative AI Hub

The generative AI hub incorporates generative AI into your AI activities in SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad

 Access

 

Augment Your SAP BTP Use Cases With AI Foundation on SAP BTP

AI Foundation is the starting point for SAP developers to build and extend AI applications. It includes everything you need from easily embedding business-ready AI into your application, to building your own custom AI models from scratch.

  Replay

 

 

Infuse Generative AI into your SAP BTP Based Extensions

SAP Business AI is SAP’s answer to infuse the power of Artificial Intelligence into SAP and partner applications, including the recent breakthroughs in the space of generative AI. SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) provides the underlying AI Foundation, that SAP and our partner ecosystem can benefit from in order to build innovative AI-infused extensions and applications.

  Replay

 

 

 

Certification for Partner AI Applications on SAP BTP available for FREE

SAP Integration and Certification Center (SAP ICC) is committed to support partners in the adoption of SAP Business AI and build innovate AI Apps. To further assist in these efforts, we are excited to introduce a limited-time promotion, providing free certification for partner AI applications until the end of 2024, as long as the application meets the certification requirements.

 

   Access

RISE with SAP Transformation

Overview

  Register/Access

Competency & Specialization

 

The competency framework evolves SAP’s recognition of its partner ecosystem expertise by categorizing partners by the competency and specialization they offer the market giving customers transparency on the skills, expertise, and maturity of a partner’s SAP practice. The competencies and specializations are aligned to SAP’s cloud portfolio and are cloud-focused.

 

   Access

Requirements for the Competency Framework

 

Partner performance and achievement are key drivers for attaining a competency or specialization designations. The requirements include three key pillar areas: Knowledge, Customer Success and Proficiency.

   Access

Explore SAP for Professional Services

 

Deliver client value and sustain growth for professional services. Meet client expectations, generate new revenue streams, offer digital services, and adopt new outcome-based business models for nonlinear growth by integrating data and processes through digital transformation and managing your total workforce in the cloud.

 

  Access

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Sales Tools & Assets

 

Access the SAP S/4HANA Cloud sales resources, success stories, and competitive insights to succeed in the field.

 

   Access

SAP Introduces a New Validation Recognition for Service Partners

To support customers’ RISE with SAP transformations, SAP is training and validating partners to use RISE with SAP Methodology and collaborating closely with these partners on quality and consistency. By following the methodology, customers can drive predictable timelines for implementations and provide progress transparency with key milestone assessments.

 

    Access

Customer Success Excellence Hub

 

Customer success is partner success. SAP and partners together delivering customer success. Customer success is all about creating the best customer experience – supporting customers to continuously realize value from their SAP cloud investment, delivering a simple consistent experience across SAP and partners. Customer success is a key part of everyone’s role – from sales through to service and support. Investing in customer success is an investment in growth.

 

    Access

Guide: RISE with SAP - Qualified Partner Cloud Architect Access Management Request

This asset enables partners on how to raise a request for system/tool access to key private cloud process tools, including SAP Work Zone and the RISE with SAP pre-provisioning form BTP application

 

    Access

News from SAP Activate - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408, RISE with SAP, and more

We are pleased to announce the availability of the updates to SAP Activate. This release significantly enhances the methodology guidance across a wide range of products and solution-specific content. These updates aim to streamline and improve the implementation process, providing a more seamless experience for our valued users. Here are the key highlights for this release. You can learn more in the detailed sections below.

 

 

     Access

RISE with SAP: Qualification and recognition for partners

Enhance your skills, increase your presence, and solidify your credibility in the marketplace by achieving designation and recognition available to SAP partners who deliver implementation services for RISE with SAP.

 

     Access

SAP Private Cloud Metering

SAP Private Cloud Metering provides full transparency on the SAP Private Cloud consumption with monthly updated metering data. The metering data is made available in SAP for Me so that license gaps can be identified and clarified in a timely and product-specific manner.

 

     Access

SAP S/4HANA Customer Evolution Program

 

The SAP S/4HANA Customer Evolution Program is designed to help our ERP installed base customers accelerate their move to SAP S/4HANA Cloud. It comprises an end-to-end methodology providing prescriptive guidance, best practices, and automated tools and more to simplify our customer’s transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

 

     Access

Transition Brownfield Projects

This Knowledge Base Document is the central reference to additional documents focused on guidelines and information for Brownfield Projects (Migration/ Conversion or System Copy) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition.

 

      Access

      

RISE with SAP adoption framework

 

Use this pitch deck to present RISE with SAP adoption framework to your customers. The RISE with SAP adoption framework provides clarity on the entire cloud journey and brings partners and SAP together to collaborate under a single cloud operational governance model to proactively manage our common success.

 

 

    Access

 

Customer Onboarding Webcast

Select the solution of interest to access live and on demand webinars tailored to simplify your journey with SAP.

  • SAP Cloud ERP
  • SAP BTP
  • SAP SuccessFactors HCM
  • SAP Financial Management
  • SAP Training and Adoption
  • SAP Signavio and LeanIX
  • SAP Intelligent Spend and Business Network
  • SAP Supply Chain Management

    Access

 

Signavio

Overview

Register/Access

What's New in business process transformation

Learn more about Business Process Transformation latest news, recently added, and/or updated information. 

 

Access

Onboarding Resource Center: One-Stop-Shop for SAP Signavio

 

If you are a new SAP Signavio customer, it can be overwhelming to find your bearings around the variety of SAP websites, documentation and platforms. With the huge number of texts, videos and other materials available on SAP Signavio, it can be hard to find the right information. If you can find it at all.  That’s the reason we have created the Onboarding Resource Center (ORC), to give you a single access point to find all relevant resources and information about SAP Signavio – well-structured and at a glance. 

 

 Access

Exceed on procurement excellence with SAP Signavio and SAP Ariba

In today's rapidly changing business landscape, intelligent procurement has emerged as a critical component for ensuring business resilience. Achieving this requires a strong focus on processes. Join us for an insightful webcast where SAP Ariba and SAP Signavio come together to demonstrate how their combined strengths provide the fastest path to procurement excellence. Discover how a value-driven approach to process harmonization can elevate your procurement strategy and drive sustainable success

 

 Register

 

  • Session: 23rd October 2024
    09:00 AM

Discover the power of SAP Signavio Lab Space and learn how this innovation platform can contribute to a better SAP Signavio. This webcast will showcase how you can effectively engage with the platform and explore its experimental features. 

Register

    04:00 PM CEST

Navigating DORA Compliance - Achieving Digital Resilience with SAP LeanIX and SAP Signavio - US/EMEA

In this webcast, we will demonstrate how SAP LeanIX and SAP Signavio can help you meet the requirements of the Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) more efficiently and effectively. Learn how our integrated solutions support your efforts to ensure compliance and strengthen operational resilience.

 

Register

    04:00 PM CEST

 

Explore SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager is a cloud-based process transformation solution that gives companies the ability to collaboratively plan, manage, and execute transformation initiatives leading to quantifiable success. The comprehensive solution allows organizations to benefit from centralized and collaborative enhancements, make value-driven decisions, and achieve measurable business value through continuous improvements

 

Access

Understand your customers’ objectives and challenges by taking a customer-centric value selling approach. SAP LeanIX | Cloud Test, Demo, and Development

 

SAP LeanIX is a market leader for enterprise architecture, driving the modernization of IT landscapes and continuous business transformation.

 

 Access

 

 

Link the SAP Signavio Process Manager to the SAP Solution Manager - Hands on session

This webinar is a follow up to the September webinar on the interface between SAP Signavio Process Manager and SAP Solution Manager - the Business Process Model Connector. In the earlier webinar we gave an overview on the general concepts. In this hands on webinar we will focus on the actual doing in the system with an extensive demo and tips and tricks. This will help partners to leverage the existing documentation and to implement this connection. The whole session is hands on and interactive. Target group are SAP partners working with SAP Signavio and SAP Solution Manager. Focus is on delivery.

 

 Register

 

  • Session: 29th October 2024
    04:00 PM to 05:00 PM CEST

 

Explore Business Process Transformation

 

Design, Analyze and Automate improved Processes — Driving Efficiency and Customer Excellence with SAP Signavio. SAP offers a holistic suite of flexible process transformation solutions for customers to transform their business processes end-to-end with SAP Signavio solutions.

 

Access

How to use the new SAP Signavio Process Insights connector (SAP Signavio plug and gain eebinar series)

In the session, you will receive a better understanding of the newly available connector for plug and gain, enabling partners and customers to load and refresh data individually. We will outline the connector’s objectives and value proposition along with providing a detailed demo on how to actually navigate and use the connector. We will also provide additional sources, e.g.  blog entries and videos, for deeper knowledge and self-enablement.

 

 Register

 

  • Session: 10th October 2024
    04:00 PM to 05:00 PM CET

 

Unlock the secret to successful RISE with SAP Cross LoB selling and enjoy increased revenues, profits, and customer loyalty.

 

Join this enablement session to learn how the SAP Customer Evolution cross-solution area approach, resources, and tools, along with the SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite will elevate your sales engagements to a new level of productivity.

 Register

    09:00 AM to 10:00 AM CET – 04:00 PM to 05:00 PM CET

 

Partner Delivery Groups

Overview

This private group is focused solely on helping you make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition projects successful. We are dedicated to your success and work to quickly scale the latest SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition product knowledge, and solution lifecycle experiences out to the wider community. Confidently deploy and integrate AI models designed for SAP applications, cost-efficiently at scale while preserving privacy and compliance.

Access

SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network

In the SAP Ariba and Business Network Delivery Group , we excited to share that we are welcoming our Test, Demonstration & Development Program team, to the SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network Partner Delivery Group. This collaboration has allowed us to invite over 300 partner consultants who participate in the Test, Demonstration & Development Program.
