entry.title: Error message no. AIST009: Profit Center change in Investment order and Asset Under Construction.
entry.content:
In this blog I would like to explain how to change Profit Center in Investment order and Asset Under Construction. Error message no. AIST009:
Please note: In my case Profit center was not assigned to any of the cost center master data. Profit center was deriving from custom table based on Cost center But same case might useful when you change PC assignment in Cost center master data. This part I have not tested.


Here is an example of Investment Order and AUC master data.

When we try to change profit center in Asset master data in AS02:


When we try to change profit center in Investment Order master data in KO02:

Usually, Asset Under Construction is created with reference to Investment Order. As both have linked each other we cannot change profit centre individually at AUC or IO level.


Here the solution:
Step 1: Go to KO02 transaction, Change the profit center, then click on Extra and select Asset Under Construction as below.

Step 2: Then it will open AUC with edit mode. Here you change the profit center and press enter. You will get a pop up. Select the option as per your requirement. (Here I selected Yes.) and then press back or F3 and save the Investment Order.

Step 3: After saving the Investment Order, Profit Center gets updated in both IO and AUC. Below picture shows result of Profit center changed.

You might face some other issue depending on your system configuration.
Please feel free to ask addition questions if any or correct me if I am wrong.
Thank you!

SAP S/4HANA Finance

entry.title: PDM Newsletter GROW with SAP - October 2024
entry.content:
Welcome to the GROW with SAP Newsletter

We are thrilled to bring you the GROW with SAP Newsletter, your essential source for keeping up with the latest insights, trends, and innovations in the world of SAP. Each issue is carefully curated to provide you with expert advice, success stories, and practical content aimed at empowering you expanding your skills and thriving in the dynamic SAP landscape.

Whether you're eager to dive into new technologies or seeking tailored knowledge, our newsletter is your one-stop destination. We also encourage you to share this valuable resource with your colleagues and become an active member of our thriving community.

Thank you for your unwavering support! If you have any thoughts or inquiries, please don't hesitate to reach out. We're dedicated to helping you maximize your Grow with SAP experience.

Stay engaged, stay informed, and subscribe today by sending your details to beatriz.mata@sap.com

Let's grow together!

 

Beatriz Mata Chacón

beatriz.mata@sap.com 

Partner Delivery Manager EMEA South

SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Ecosystem Delivery Success | Partner Ecosystem Success

 

Top Highlights

Overview

       Register/Access

SAP Enterprise Cloud Services – Partner Summit 2024 (EMEA/MEE)

SAP Enterprise Cloud Services Partner Summit 2024' is a virtual summit to empower our SAP PartnerEdge, Sell and SAP PartnerEdge, Service partners. It will be scenario-based training comprised of some of the topics listed below.

            Register

 

  • Session: 15th October 2024
    09:00 AM to 12:00 PM CET

 

  • Session: 16th  October 2024
    09:00 AM to 11:30 PM CET

 

Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Release

The second major release of the year 2024 is here! The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release is filled with innovations that boost your productivity and support your everyday work life.

We are thrilled to give you further insights on our fully-fledged cloud ERP, supporting your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise in this blog post.

 

          Access

Webinar: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Essentials for Finance

Welcome to the implementation essentials event by expertise services on the topic of finance. This session is to support project teams preparing for, or involved in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations.

This is a 3-day event, each day is 2.5 hours, and will include the following topic areas related to the finance line of business:

  • Key finance implementation decisions will be discussed (ledger/parallel accounting/chart of accounts/fiscal year variant/currencies)
  • Update to date major innovations included (financial accounting/management accounting/financial operations/cash/group reporting/closing/AI etc.)
  • Presenters will share implementation experience and lessons learned

         Register

  • Session 1: 15th October 2024, 9:00 AM to 11:30 AM Europe/Amsterdam
  • Session 2: 16th October 2024, 9:00 AM to 11:30 AM Europe/Amsterdam
  • Session 3: 17th October 2024, 9:00 AM to 11:30 AM Europe/Amsterdam

Partner Learning Series: Optimizing SAP Portfolio in the Public Sector

The Partner Learning Series is tailored to elevate the learning experiences of our partners seeking to maximize the use of the SAP Portfolio in the Public Sector. Our on-demand sessions, each lasting approximately 20 minutes, provide a deeper understanding of transitioning government customers to the Cloud in collaboration with experienced industry experts. You'll have the flexibility to learn at your own pace about SAP's Strategy, priorities, and trends, including SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and cloud deployment options.

The series delves into SAP S/4HANA Cloud, RISE with SAP, GROW with SAP, and SAP Business AI within the Public Sector industry, with a focus on grants and funds management, SAP SuccessFactors HCM, sourcing and procurement, and citizen services. Join us as we explore new insights and strategies to drive success in the Public Sector.

        Access

 

 

 

Events and Webinars for Partners

Overview

    Register/Access

Webinar: New resource-related intercompany billing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

New resource-related Intercompany Billing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is the successor of old resource-related Intercompany Billing. This live session will provide a comprehensive overview and describe the key differentiating capabilities compared to the predecessor from a functional point of view.

     Register

  • Session 1: 1st October 2024, 10:00 AM - 11:00 AM Europe/Berlin
  • Session 2: 1st October 2024, 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM Europe/Berlin

Webinar: GROW with SAP with Concur Expense – deep-dive session

Discover the exciting opportunity we now have to offer the value of Concur Expense to our GROW with SAP premium customers. During this session we will cover:

  • GROW with SAP w/Concur entitlement & Concur partner programs
  • Concur Expense product overview and SAP S/4HANA w/Expense value prop
  • Integration and implementation
  • The opportunity and next steps

 

     Register

  • Session 1: 3rd October 2024, 09:00 AM - 10:00 AM Europe/Berlin
  • Session 2: 3rd October 2024, 5:00 PM - 6:00 PM Europe/Berlin

Webinar: Explore GROW with SAP entitlements - SAP Analytics Cloud for planning

SAP Analytics Cloud is the one planning solution combining business intelligence, augmented analytics, and built-in collaboration tools for everyone to work together and make faster, confident decisions. Learn more about them in this session

     Register

  • Session 1: 4th October 2024,  12:00 AM - 13:00 AM SGT

Webinar: SAP Supply Chain Management for GROW with SAP

This session will provide you with an overview of GROW with SAP, and how SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition helps companies with the embedded Supply Chain Management capabilities. The focus of the session is to introduce the complementing solutions from the SAP Supply Chain Management portfolio. These solutions integrate with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and provide a perfect upsell opportunity for customers who have specific requirements in Supply Chain Management processes. You will learn about how to position these solutions in the GROW with SAP context, and about customer success stories.

     Register

 

  • Session 1: 7th October 2024, 4:00 PM GMT - 5:00 PM Europe/Berlin

 

 

Webinar: Introduction to SAP Information Lifecycle Management (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Hot Topic Series)

During this session our experts will provide insights on the "Why" and "How" of SAP Information Lifecycle Management. We will discuss possible use-cases and provide an overview of features and functions. Additionally, we will showcase the differences between SAP ILM in an SAP S/4HANA on-prem or private cloud deployment vs. an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition deployment.  For example, you will learn about the SAP ILM advisor which provides guidance for the data volume aspect of SAP ILM and get to know about archive file storage infrastructure. 

 

     Register

  • Session 1: 8th October 2024,  10:00 AM - 11:00 AM Europe/Amsterdam

 

Webinar: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Data Migration Fundamentals

In this workshop, you will gain fundamental knowledge on the data migration workstream in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition based on SAP Activate methodology.

 

This workshop will start with an introduction to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Expertise Services Community, and we will take you through the fundamental aspects of data migration and the migration cockpit for SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

It will introduce the migration cockpit, data readiness, cutover as using the 3 system landscape for your data migration workstream. In the end we will also share some lessons learned on data migration and provide some space for question and answers.

 

     Register

  • Session 2: 8th October 2024, 04:00 PM - 05:30 PM CET

Webinar: Expand the value of GROW with SAP with add-on packages for chief financial officer, chief supply chain officer and chief human resources officer

In this session we will review currently offered GROW with SAP packages in financial management, digital supply chain and human resources. We will update you on the changes since the packages were launched earlier this year. You will also have an opportunity to ask questions.

      Register

  • Session: 9th October 2024, 09:00 AM - 10:30 AM CET

 

Webinar: SAP Project and Resource Management for GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (deep dive)

After the training you will know how SAP Project and Resource Management integrates with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Professional Services scenario. How project staffing is done, which apps are provided and the key capabilities. Further you will learn which integration scenarios are available and how to proceed to setup your integration scenario..

       Register

  • Session 1: 9th October 2024, 02:30 PM - 05:00 PM Asia/Singapore

 

Webinar: Explore GROW with SAP Entitlements – SAP Ariba Buying, SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

GROW with SAP entitlements offer support for customers looking to quickly and predictably adopt cloud ERP with continuous innovation. SAP Ariba Buying, base edition creates a smart and simple purchasing experience that increases user engagement. SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management on SAP Business Technology Platform is seamlessly integrated to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and provides a single point of access and visibility to supplier invoices from multiple connected systems. Learn more about them in this session

 

       Register

  • Session 1: 9th October 2024, 12:00 PM - 01:00 PM Asia/Singapore

Webinar: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Integration & Extensibility Fundamentals

In this interactive webinar, you will gain understanding on aspects & capabilities of Integration & Extensibility within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This webinar will start with an introduction to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud expertise services community, outlining the importance Integration & Extensibility in SAP Activate methodology, integration types and extensibility types – followed with an opportunity to interact and raise questions.

       Register

  • Session 1: 28th October 2024, 04:00 PM CET - 05:30 PM CET

Webinar: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Essentials for Sourcing & Procurement 

Welcome to the Implementation Essentials event by Expertise Services on the topic of Sourcing & Procurement. This session is to support Partner project teams preparing for or involved in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations.

        Register

  • Session 1: 23rd October 2024, 3:00 PM - 5:00 PM Europe/Amsterdam
  • Session 2: 24th October 2024, 3:00 PM - 5:00 PM Europe/Amsterdam

 

Transformation with S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Overview

    Register/Access

Find Release Information & Content with the Release Navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Join us for an interactive virtual live session where we’ll walk you through the features and benefits of the Release Navigator. Discover how this tool provides a comprehensive view of upcoming releases, allowing you to easily plan and prepare for updates. You’ll also learn how to find release scheduling and maintenance windows within the release navigator, enabling you to better plan for and manage the deployment of updates.

 

       Access

Advanced Solution Bundling for Solution Order Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Check out the latest blog post on our newsletter about the new features introduced in Solution Order Management with the rollout of the 2402 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This update supports the transition towards solution-based business models, offering a broad scope and range of customizable offerings. Find out more about how these business models drive competitive advantage and how the new solution bundles functionality in the 2402.3 release enables more efficient selling and processing of holistic solutions. Don't miss out on this insightful read.

 

       Access

Transportation Management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408

Don't miss out on the latest product updates for Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408! We are thrilled to introduce the integration of Returns Purchase Orders with Transportation Management, enabling you to seamlessly perform transportation planning, monitor execution, and settle freight costs for returns purchase orders as part of the Return to Supplier process (scope item BMK). Stay tuned for more details on these exciting features.

 

       Access

 

 

Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408

Discover the latest innovations in Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management with the release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. Our blog post outlines key topics, including reserving material using project demand (4RF), creating a project by copying an existing project using an API (1NT, 1YF), and integration with Project Plan Collaboration (6JN). Stay updated and explore how these innovations can elevate your project management experience.

 

       Access

 

 

 

Artificial Intelligence & Technology in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 

Check out the latest blog post on Artificial Intelligence and Technologies, highlighting the newest features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2408. Our release introduces a diverse range of AI-driven functionalities that are designed to elevate user experience, enhance operational efficiency, and foster better decision-making. With features such as Joule, AI-assisted financial insights, and intelligent resource matching, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release 2408 promises to optimize your business operations, making them more intuitive, accurate, and efficient. We extend our utmost appreciation to our dedicated developers for their hard work and commitment

 

        Access

SAP Recommendation: Assignment of Corporate Accounting Principle to Ledger

Check out our latest blog post, which provides guidance on the SAP Recommendation for assigning Corporate Accounting Principles to the Ledgers for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition (Public Cloud). The blog explains each scenario, outlines the future impacts if the recommendation is not followed, and discusses when it is possible to change the Corporate Accounting Principal assignment. Gain valuable insights into best practices for efficient Corporate Accounting Principal assignment in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

entry.title: Upgrading SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Why, How, and Best Practices guide - Version 4
entry.content:
Considering upgrading to SAP S/4HANA 2023 or higher?

The latest version of our well-known supplementary guide is now released. You can use this guide to:

  • Build your business case for upgrade
  • Plan your upgrade
  • Execute your upgrade - supplementing the official SAP S/4HANA upgrade guide for your release + solution suite
  • Get more out of your SAP S/4HANA solution post-upgrade

Now including even more lessons learned from real customers, latest tooling to make your upgrades more efficient, plus pragmatic advice on what to do with upgrade versus post-upgrade. 

Hint: Our most important recommendation is to do some pre-planning of post-upgrade adoption. This makes sure your technical upgrade has considered any critical architecture prerequisites to smooth your post-upgrade plans, so that you gain the promised business value of your new SAP S/4HANA release. 

You can download the guide here:

Upgrading SAP S/4HANA - Why, How, and Best Practices

What's new in Version 4

The guide is now focussed on:

  • Solution suite SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, with some additional comments for SAP S/4HANA on-premise customers.
  • Project approaches Technical Upgrade and Technical Upgrade Plus (i.e. with carefully chosen urgent new business value) 
  • Release SAP S/4HANA 2023 and higher 
  • Clean Core - what's mandatory for your upgrade 
  • Recommended tooling to make your upgrades more efficient - including SAP Signavio, SAP CALM, SAP Activate, and more
  • Advice on SAP Services that may help you manage your upgrade
  • Known issues and FAQs
  • Practical advice on what major shifts to include with your upgrade versus defer to post-upgrade

The guide has been restructured to clarify what activities to include in your Plan, Execute, and Post-Upgrade. 

In the Plan chapter:

For those considering using their upgrade as an opportunity to deal with major shifts - including compatibility packs expiring end of 2025, or end-of-maintenance SAP Business Suite solutions expiring end of 2027 - we have added a series of Special Considerations.  The aim is to give you the critical notes and references to start evaluating your options, assess business impact, and decide whether to make the shift with upgrade or post-upgrade. 

In the Execute chapter:

We have included mandatory activities and recommended activities for technical upgrades. Recommended activities are those that improve the efficiency of your upgrades and of your business-as-usual support. 

In the Post-Upgrade chapter: 

We have added some advice to get you started on introducing new business value whether you want to:

  • Move to Clean Core
  • Introduce new business processes
  • Introduce new processes powered by SAP Business AI
  • Introduce new user experiences - more apps, more intelligent experiences, central launchpad on SAP Build Zone, mobile devices and/or wearables, transition users from SAP GUI to SAP Fiori, and more
  • Make important shifts that help you transition your users to innovation over time, such as move from groups to spaces and pages

And finally there are updated learning recommendations for users, functional consultants, UX architects, developers, and administrators. 

We hope you find the guide useful for your upgrade planning... and we always welcome feedback!

Important: This is intended to be the final version of this guide (we may perhaps make some minor corrections).  Future advice is being collected into the SAP Activate roadmaps for upgrading SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, and upgrading SAP S/4HANA on-premise.

Looking for more ... ?

You’ll find much more on our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition topic page and  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

We also recommend you follow tags SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates.

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits – New Migration Guide LE-WM to EWM on S/4HANA
entry.content:
In this blog, I would like to share the latest updates on the new migration guide that was released for supporting customers and consultants during planning the migration from SAP ERP LE-WM or SAP S/4HANA Stock Room Management to EWM on S/4HANA. 

Additionally, I would like to clear up some confusion about end dates of usage rights and mainstream maintenance for SAP LE-WM.

LE-WM Roadmap - Important Dates:
Let's start with the SAP LE-WM roadmap and timeline, as this is mainly dependent on the ERP solution release on which LE-WM is running.

  • LE-WM on ERP ECC:
    SAP will provide mainstream maintenance until 31.12.2027 for SAP Business Suite 7 core applications, refer to SAP Note 2881788 for details.

  • LE-WM on ERP S/4HANA:
    SAP restricts the usage of components which are in a compatibility mode until 31.12.2025, refer to SAP Notes 2269324, 2881166, and 2577428 for details.

Migration Guide Overview:
In this migration guide you will find information about:

  • General Process Changes between LE-WM and EWM
  • System Setup Guides
  • Organizational Structure
  • Customizing Settings for Product Master
  • Define Handling Unit Management
  • Storage Bin Migration
  • Putaway and Stock Removal
  • Material Master Migration
  • Transactional Data Migration
  • Stock Migration
  • Physical Inventory Completeness Migration

Out of Scope:

  • In general, transactional data is not transferred from LE-WM to EWM. So no documents can be open when the migration starts. For more information, see Transactional Data Migration.
  • Existing RF transactions are not transferred automatically from LE-WM to EWM.
  • System users and the related authorization roles and profiles are not transferred automatically from LE-WM to EWM.
  • The existing migration tools do not migrate Customizing. Due to the broader functionality in EWM, the Customizing differs from LE-WM. For more information, see General Process Changes between LE-WM and EWM.
  • Customer-specific objects are not transferred

SAP Note link where you can download the mgiration guide: https://me.sap.com/notes/3523591.

Thanks to the EWM product management and CoE teams for the preparation of this useful document.

Learn more about SAP Premium Engagement and how a global team of experts can help you to get the most out of your SAP solutions.

If you already are a Premium Engagement customer and you are interested in knowing more about SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain Extended Warehouse Management solution, contact your Technical Quality Manager (TQM) to arrange for Architecture Point of View (APoV) service.

#CoE EMEA SCM1

entry.title: Test System Refresh from Production copy with GDPR compliance
entry.content:
In today’s fast-evolving digital landscape, businesses rely on robust and up-to-date test systems to develop, test, and deploy new features and applications seamlessly. Also training staff using a training system which has data like production system, accelerates digital transformation and user adoption of new feature and software products.

One of the most efficient ways to achieve this is by performing a Test System Refresh using production data. However, organizations need to ensure that this process adheres to stringent data protection laws like the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in Europe, ICO in UK, CCPA, PIPEDA in Americas, DPDPA in India and many others similar across the globe. When done correctly, production copy for tests and training offers multiple business benefits, enhancing both operational efficiency and compliance. In this post we explore, why refreshing test and training systems from production copies is a game-changer and how regulatory compliance plays a critical role in the process.

What is a Test System Refresh?

A Test System Refresh is the process of copying data from a live production environment to a test or development system. This refresh ensures that the data used for testing is as realistic as possible, reflecting the most current operational conditions. The result? Your test environments are well-aligned with production, enabling better testing, bug-fixing, and development of new features.

Why Refresh Test Systems with Production Data?

Using production data for testing offers several advantages:

  1. Realistic Data Scenarios: When developers and testers work with data that closely resembles real-world production data, the accuracy of tests improves dramatically. Scenarios can be tested with actual workflows, ensuring that any bugs or issues identified in testing are likely to be encountered in the real system as well. This leads to fewer surprises when rolling out new updates or features.
  2. Comprehensive Test Coverage: Production data is often far more complex than synthetic or dummy data. It includes various edge cases that might not have been considered during test data generation. By refreshing test systems from production, you ensure that your testing is comprehensive, reducing the risk of missing critical bugs or issues.
  3. Accelerated Development Cycles: Having accurate and up-to-date test environments allows development teams to work faster and more efficiently. Issues are easier to identify, troubleshoot, and resolve when developers are working in a realistic setting. This leads to shorter development cycles, faster feature releases, and improved time-to-market.
  4. Cost Savings: The ability to test with production data reduces the number of errors that slip into production. Fixing issues in production is far more expensive than addressing them during testing. By investing in regular test system refreshes, businesses can significantly reduce operational costs over time.
  5. Reduced Training Costs: When onboarding new employees or training existing ones on new features, having access to test systems that closely mirror production is invaluable. Realistic data helps employees understand the systems better, improving training efficiency and reducing the time it takes for employees to become fully productive.

 

GDPR and other regulatory Compliance: The Crucial Piece of the Puzzle

While refreshing test systems with production data offers significant benefits, it’s critical to ensure that this process complies with GDPR. The regulation mandates strict rules on how personal data should be handled, especially when it is transferred or copied to non-production systems. Failing to adhere to GDPR can result in hefty fines and damage to a company’s reputation.

So, how can businesses enjoy the benefits of production data in testing while staying compliant? Key Steps for GDPR-Compliant Test System Refreshes

  1. Data Anonymization and Pseudonymization: The most effective way to achieve GDPR compliance is by anonymizing or pseudonymizing sensitive personal data before transferring it to test environments. Anonymization ensures that the data can no longer be traced back to an individual, while pseudonymization removes the ability to link data to specific people unless the key to the pseudonymization is revealed. This protects individual privacy while allowing test environments to maintain realistic datasets.
  2. Role-Based Access Controls: Only authorized personnel should have access to the data in test environments. Implementing strict access controls and logging ensures that sensitive data is protected and that any potential breaches can be traced and contained.
  3. Data Masking: Sensitive fields such as names, addresses, or financial data can be masked during the copy process. While the data retains its structure and characteristics, the specific details are obscured, ensuring that developers and testers work with data that resembles real information but doesn't compromise individual privacy.
  4. Encryption: Ensure that all data copied from production to test systems is encrypted both at rest and in transit. This minimizes the risk of data being intercepted or stolen during the refresh process.
  5. Audit and Monitor: Regular auditing and monitoring of test environments ensure ongoing compliance with GDPR. Track who accesses the data, what changes are made, and any potential breaches or non-compliance issues.

 Business Benefits of GDPR-Compliant Test System Refresh

  1. Accelerated Digital Transformation: Digital transformation adoption is accelerated by reduced test cycles, reduced development cycles, and reduced training time for new users onboarding new digital systems.
  2. High satisfaction by end users: Availability of production copy for training and testing systems, leads to higher adoption of new features. It helps the users to adopt software and leave using old tools. User satisfaction being higher helps overall delivery of projects by IT teams.
  3. Enhanced Security: By adhering to GDPR requirements, you significantly reduce the risk of data breaches, which could result in both financial and reputational damage.
  4. Faster Compliance Audits: Regularly performing GDPR-compliant test system refreshes helps streamline your organization’s compliance audits. Having a structured, secure process in place for handling test data demonstrates a proactive approach to data protection, making compliance checks smoother.
  5. Increased Customer Trust for organisation: When customers know that their data is handled with care and protected, they are more likely to trust your organisation. A GDPR-compliant data refresh strategy ensures customer trust is maintained.

 Conclusion

Refreshing test systems with production data can revolutionise how businesses approach testing, development, and training. The use of realistic data not only boosts productivity and reduces operational costs, but it also ensures higher quality in software releases. However, the benefits of a test system refresh are only fully realized when GDPR compliance is a core part of the process. By anonymizing, encrypting, and securing test data, businesses can unlock the potential of test system refreshes while protecting individual privacy and staying compliant with the law.

By striking this balance, companies can accelerate innovation, improve operational efficiency, and ensure data protection—ultimately driving long-term business success.

entry.title: Updates on Conhecimento de Transporte Eletrônico: Simplified CT-e
entry.content:
Versão em português 

Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound invoicing option for Brazil team has released updates on the Conhecimento de Transporte Eletrônico (CT-e). The update complies with SEFAZ Technical Note 2024.002 1.01 Version - Simplified CT-e. This new version is planned to be in production on October 7, 2024, according to the tax authority.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance - CT-e Technical Note 2024.002 1.01 Version (Simplified CT-e)

The CT-e Basic and CT-e Flex processes now support the Simplified CT-e XML file. You can implement these changes in the inbound invoicing option for Brazil by following the instructions on the SAP Note 3519421 and the SAP Note 3519422.

Check out the latest updates and features in our What's New session on the SAP Help Portal:

  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP ERP

For a step-by-step guide on how to use the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound and outbound invoicing options for Brazil, refer to the following SAP Help Portal pages:

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound invoicing option for Brazil
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR (Inbound invoicing option for Brazil)
  • LOD-LH-NFE (Outbound invoicing option for Brazil)

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “like” (kudos), share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content. You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance community to keep up with the latest NF-e news.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago

entry.title: Atualizações no Conhecimento de Transporte Eletrônico: CT-e simplificado
entry.content:
English version here 

Olá pessoal,

A equipe SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil entregou atualizações no Conhecimento de Transporte Eletrônico (CT-e). A atualização está conforme a Nota Técnica 2024.002 versão 1.01 da SEFAZ (CT-e simplificado). Essa nova versão entrará em produção em 7 de outubro de 2024, conforme a autoridade fiscal.

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance - Nota Técnica 2024.002 versão 1.01 da SEFAZ (CT-e simplificado)

Os processos CT-e Básico e CT-e Flex suportam agora o arquivo XML de CT-e simplificado. Você pode implementar essas modificações na opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil seguindo as instruções na SAP Note 3519421 e também na SAP Note 3519422.

Confira as últimas atualizações e recursos na sessão What's New no portal SAP Help:

  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP ERP

Para um passo a passo sobre como usar o SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada e de faturamento de saída para o Brasil, consulte as seguintes páginas do portal SAP Help:

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil

Você pode receber alerta por email quando novas SAP Notes forem lançadas ou atualizadas nos componentes listados abaixo; basta seguir as instruções do SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR (opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil)
  • LOD-LH-NFE (opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil)

Utilize o gerenciador de modificações legais para ter uma noção do que está planejado para ser suportado. Confira o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 e a página Regulatory Change Manager para entender como usar essa ferramenta.

Gostou desse post? Dê um “like” (kudos) e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. Sinta-se à vontade para deixar suas perguntas no SAP Community e ler posts semelhantes no blogging space do SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. Para acompanhar as últimas notícias da NF-e, siga a tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) e a comunidade SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Obrigado e até a próxima,
Thiago

entry.title: Maximizing ROI from Your SAP EWM Investment: Expert Strategies for Continuous Optimization
entry.content:
Logistics Directors,  IT Program managers,  wonder if they are getting the most out of your SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) investment. If you feel like your warehouse operations aren’t fully optimized, you’re not alone. IT and logistics leaders often find themselves wondering if they’re truly maximizing ROI from their SAP systems. As a veteran with over three decades of experience in SAP implementations, I've seen countless organizations struggle to maximize their Return on Investment (ROI) from this powerful tool. In this article, we'll explore common challenges and proven strategies to unlock the full potential of your SAP EWM investment.

The SAP EWM ROI Challenge

Many organizations face similar hurdles when it comes to optimizing their SAP EWM systems:

  1. Inefficient processes that fail to leverage EWM's advanced capabilities
  2. Low user adoption due to inadequate training and support
  3. Limited data visibility, hindering informed decision-making

These issues aren't just minor setbacks – they're ROI killers. They result in slower order fulfillment, wasted resources, and widespread frustration across teams.

Expert Strategies for SAP EWM Optimization

Drawing from my 30+ years of experience, here are four key strategies to boost your SAP EWM ROI:

1. Harness Advanced Features

Task & Resource Management, RF Framework, Queue Management,  Slotting, Dynamic routing with POSC ( Process Oriented Storage Control) and LOSC ( Layout Oriented Storage Control),  Clustered Putaway, Wave Management,  Fixed Bin replenishment, Full pallet picking, Warehouse Monitor aren't just buzzwords. When properly implemented, they're game-changers for efficiency.

2. Conduct Regular Process Reviews

Your warehouse operations are dynamic, and your processes should be too. Implement quarterly workflow assessments to identify and eliminate inefficiencies.  SAP is heavily investing in EWM to roll out  new functionality with every release.  It is important to keep track of what is new and  what is relevant for continuous innovation of your EWM system.  Check this information at  SAP S/4HANA What’s New Viewer. Link to the viewer on SAP website is - https://help.sap.com/whats-new/5fc51e30e2744f168642e26e0c1d9be1  Please see below screenshot to filter it to EWM specific innovations.

 

 This ongoing optimization ensures your EWM system evolves with your business needs.

3. Prioritize Continuous Training

A one-time training session during implementation isn't enough. Develop a continuous learning program to keep your team proficient with SAP EWM. This approach not only improves system adoption but also empowers employees to leverage advanced features effectively. Encourage your subject matter experts (SME) to identify areas where users are making more mistakes and  focus on building focused training sessions on  those topics. These could be  scheduled as lunch and learn sessions as well without too much of training overheads.

4. Integrate Predictive Analytics

Combine SAP EWM with S/4HANA to unlock the power of real-time insights and predictive modeling. Some good examples could be open purchase orders / inbound deliveries / outbound deliveries to identify workload on warehouse. This integration can transform your decision-making  and planning capabilities, leading to smarter resource allocation and improved operational efficiency.

Remember, in the fast-paced world of logistics and supply chain management, standing still is equivalent to moving backward. It's time to optimize your SAP EWM investment and drive real, measurable ROI.

Are you facing challenges with your SAP EWM implementation? Share your experiences in the comments below, and let's discuss how to overcome them!

Shivaji Patil

SAP Warehousing Platinum Advisor

Genius Business Solutions, Inc.

entry.title: Cyber Security Protection for S/4HANA Backups with Enterprise Blockchain and SAP BTP Kyma 🚀
entry.content:
Welcome to the latest blog in the series on SAP and Enterprise Blockchain.

Hopefully you've all had a great summer and are full of energy and ready to go for the sprint to the end of the year, so let's bring all of our energy and get great things done.

In my journey with Enterprise Blockchain, I was talking to a CSP Telco in Norway who came with this Enterprise Blockchain use case which was new for me and a good one. It is easy to implement and extremely effective, difficulty is easy and impact is high, what's not to like 🙂 They've implemented it, by the way, and it works.

So, what's the scoop ?

As I previously wrote here Cyber Security and Resilience are no long about High Availability and Disaster Recovery, our HyperScalers have all of the capacity we could ever need and can spin up capacity on demand.

The threat these days is less that Hackers wants to steal our Data, and more that Hackers wants to simply mess up our Data so that our Business cannot Operate, rather than stealing they just want to put a spanner in the works  so to speak.

 

Credit: https://funkyenglish.com/idiom-throw-spanner-works/

 

Just imagine if a Hacker messed up the Data of an Airline, planes wouldn't be able to fly, if a Hacker messed up the Data of a Pharmaceutical company, there would be no medicines, if a Hacker messed up the Data of a Groceries company, there would be nothing to eat.

 

What's the Risk ?

In the same way that Fire Fighters prepare for fires, having the latest equipment and training, and assuming unfortunately that there will one day be a fire...

As part of our BCP Business Continuity Planning we must assume that one day our Organisation will be the victim of a Hacker, and the Operational SAP Applications will be down and will need to be Restored from Backup.

And here's the question, how can we be sure that the Hacker hasn't messed with our Backup File(s) ?

You want to Restore from Backup, but there is a doubt that the Hacker has messed with the Backup File, how can you be sure, which Backup file you can trust as the good one ?

And this is the Risk, can you Trust the originality and integrity of your Backup file(s) ?

 

What's the Solution ?

How can we guarantee that we know whether or not the Backup File(s) have been messed with ?

This is easy, we think about our use case or our demand and we look in our Enterprise Technology Standards for the technology which is the most suited to guaranteeing that Data can be trusted. 

And we find our Enterprise Blockchain Technology Standard.

So how can we use our Enterprise Blockchain which is running on the SAP BTP Kyma to guarantee that we know if we can trust the integrity and originality of our Backup files ?

This is so easy it's like boiling an egg,

tl;dr; we run a Hash over the Backup file and store the Hash output in the Enterprise Blockchain where it is super safely protected thanks to the special characteristics of the Blockchain (Immutable, Hash Mechanism, Distributed, Consensus Mechanism), and perish the thought, in the event of a BCP scenario, where we need to Recover with a Backup, we run a new Hash over the Backup file and compare the new Hash to the old Hash (which is safely  stored in the Blockchain to protect the integrity and originality of the Hash) and we see if the two Hashes are the same, if they are the same we are good to go with the Restore from Backup, if they are different then we go to the next Backup File and do the same again until we find a Backup File that we can trust.

And the long answer for those of us who are interested in the finer details...

Voila.... bring in the Enterprise Blockchain Platform Wallet. The best Enterprise Blockchain Platform products include what is called the Enterprise Blockchain Platform Wallet, or to make it shorter, the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet.

The Enterprise Blockchain Wallet enables us to store large Data, like large Files safely and securely off the chain, or 'Off-Chain'. 

But if we store the large Data files Off-Chain in the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet, then how do we also have them some how on the Enterprise Blockchain Database ?

The way this works is elegant, in any decent Enterprise Blockchain Platform, the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet location is completely configurable, and could be anywhere from SAP HANA Cloud (Data Lake), or for example multiple hyperscaler object stores, such as Amazon S3, OSS (Alicloud Object Storage Service), SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake, and Azure Blob Storage.

The configurable Enterprise Blockchain Wallet of the Enterprise Blockchain Platform looks like this:

 

Enterprise Blockchain running on SAP BTP Kyma with Enterprise Blockchain Wallet

 

Ok, so we've got the Backup File stored in the (configurable) Enterprise Blockchain Wallet, but what about securing the Backup File Data ? Obviously the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet storage location has built in security, for example the SAP HANA Cloud, the AWS S3 Buckets, but we need more than the out of the box security of these products, the reason we are using the Enterprise Blockchain Database is because of the amazing security strengths that it natively out of the box has, and so, what about the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet, doesn't the Enterprise Blockchain Platform have some cool super hard way of protecting the data in the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet ?

Well yes it does, this is the magic of Enterprise Blockchain Database 'Off-Chain' storage in the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet. This is so unique to Blockchain Technologies.

What happens is this, when store data in the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet, the Enterprise Blockchain Platform software runs a hash algorithm over the data that we have stored and the data, and the large file gets hashed:

 

 

The Backup File in the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet gets Hashed, and then, that Hash is stored in the Enterprise Blockchain Database.

This means we now have a unique Hash of that Backup File, and if anybody or anything makes even the tiniest teeniest change to that data or file, next time we run a Hash over that data or file the result will be different that the original Hash which is safely stored in the Enterprise Blockchain Database and this is how we will know that the data has been changed and we cannot trust the Data and therefore we cannot use it for Restoring our System.

On the other hand, if just before we plan to run the Restore from Backup with the Backup File from the Enterprise Blockchain Wallet, if we run a Hash over the Data and the Hash result is the same as we have in the Hash which is stored safely in Enterprise Blockchain Database, then we will know we can trust the Backup File and we can use it for our System Restore from Backup.

 

Enterprise Blockchain running on SAP BTP Kyma protecting SAP S/4HANA Backup Integrity

 

 

And this is why, for all of these reasons, 

Trustable S/4HANA Backups depend of storing a Hash of the Backup File in the  Enterprise Blockchain

 

Enterprise Blockchain is:

. The Digital Transformation of Information Security 

. The Next Generation Data Integrity, Originality, Confidentiality Protection

. Re-imagining Information Security

. Natively, out of the box, due to its special characteristics the strongest, hardest, most resilient Enterprise Database product 

 

Enterprise Blockchain is both:

. a Secure Store

. a Secure Communication Channel

 

The configurable Enterprise Blockchain Wallet enables you to store Big Data 'Off-Chain' and the hashes of the Big Data are stored safely and securely on the Enterprise Blockchain Database.

And this is Why I love SAP and Blockchain Databases and why you should too 🚀

The good news is, as we discussed in the previous blog, this is no longer hype, we can do all of this today, and now, within the SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem there are enabling technology Blockchain Products designed and built by SAP Experts specifically for the needs of SAP Customers to make doing Blockchain and SAP easy, and so you can do SAP and Blockchain, today it's real and there's nothing stopping you. Here's a how to guide which you can use to try this today with the SAP BTP Kyma Trial and Enterprise Blockchain.

So what are we waiting for ? Oh yeah, more use cases, ok, that will be the next blog. 

What do you think, are the words AI, Blockchain, Web3, Distributed Ledger Technology, starting to appear in your Company's visions and technology visions ? What use cases are you looking at ? Let's chat about it in the comments.

For now, over and out.

Andy Silvey.

Independent SAP Technical Architect and CEO of atkrypto

Author Bio:

Andy Silvey is a 25 years SAP Technology veteran [15 years SAP Basis and 10 years SAP Tech Arch including Tech, Integration, Security, Data from 3.1H to S/4HANA PCE on RISE and the BTP and everything in between, and former SCN Moderator and Mentor alumni].

Andy is also co-Founder of atkrypto inc, an startup whose ambition is to make Blockchain easy for Enterprise.

atkrypto's flagship product is the atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP,  and atkrypto is a SAP Partner Edge Open EcoSystem Partner. 

The atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has been designed by SAP Independent Experts for the needs of SAP Customers and to be deployed on the SAP BTP Kyma Runtime Service and leverage native integration to SAP Products.

atkrypto Enterprise Blockchain Platform for SAP has a number of unique qualities, including being the only Blockchain software in the world which has a DataCenter version and a light mobile version which can run on Edge/IoT/Mobile devices and enables data to be written to the Blockchain at the Edge where that same Blockchain is running on a Server in the DataCenter, protecting the integrity and originality of data from the Edge to Insights. Taking Blockchain to the Data at the Edge instead of taking the Data to the Blockchain.
